_purpose: Courthouse Atlas for Dent County, MO. Record survival and courthouse history. Dent County formed 1851. No major courthouse fire — records relatively well-preserved from county formation. Salem is the county seat. Adjacent to the Old Lead Belt — some mining company records supplement county records.

previous_courthouses.fate: Early courthouse, replaced as county grew. Records transferred.
Replaced by the current WPA-era building in 1940. Records transferred intact.

record_losses.cause: Civil War guerrilla warfare disrupted record-keeping
records_survived: Most county records survived; courthouse not burned
record_losses.description: The Civil War disrupted record-keeping in Dent County. Guerrilla activity made court sessions irregular. Some records from the war years are incomplete. The courthouse itself was not destroyed.
record_losses.narrative_impact: Records from 1861-1865 may have gaps — the guerrilla war made holding court an act of courage, and recording transactions was secondary to survival.
record_losses.source: Missouri State Archives. Guide to County Records on Microfilm: Dent County.

deeds.gaps: Civil War-era recording may be incomplete.
deeds.repository: Dent County Recorder of Deeds, Salem; Missouri State Archives

wills_estates.completeness: Good — from county formation. Sparse in the earliest years.
wills_estates.gaps: Few estates probated on the frontier.
wills_estates.repository: Dent County Probate Court; Missouri State Archives

marriages.repository: Dent County Recorder; Missouri State Archives

court_minutes.completeness: Good — from county formation. Civil War-era sessions irregular.
court_minutes.gaps: Irregular court sessions during the Civil War.
court_minutes.repository: Missouri State Archives; Dent County Circuit Court

vital_records.completeness: Poor compliance in rural areas until approximately 1910. Salem compliance better than the surrounding hollows.
vital_records.repository: Missouri Department of Health and Senior Services; Missouri State Archives

substitute_sources.source_type: Iron furnace and mining company records
substitute_sources.description: Records from the iron furnaces in eastern Dent County and adjacent lead/zinc operations may document workers and families in the industrial economy.
substitute_sources.repository: Missouri Historical Society, St. Louis; State Historical Society of Missouri; Missouri Department of Natural Resources historical mining records
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Limited survival, but where records exist they document workers — including enslaved workers at the iron furnaces — who may not appear in other records.
substitute_sources.source: Rafferty, The Ozarks: Land and Life (2001).

substitute_sources.description: The Dent County Republican and other Salem papers documented the community from the 1870s.
substitute_sources.repository: State Historical Society of Missouri; Dent County Library
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Good supplement. Court reporting, death notices, and property transfer announcements.
substitute_sources.source: State Historical Society of Missouri newspaper holdings.

substitute_sources.description: Population schedules 1860-1940. First enumerated as Dent County in 1860.

narrative_hooks: Dent County's courthouse survived the Civil War — one of the lucky ones. If {ancestor_name} was in the county from 1851, the records are likely intact.
The iron furnace records in eastern Dent County may document workers the county records never mentioned — if {ancestor_name} worked at a furnace, the company's books may be the only evidence.

Missouri vital records were required from 1883, but in the hollows around Salem, nobody came to ask — if {ancestor_name} was born in a cabin on the Current River headwaters, the birth may never have been registered.

Dent County sits at the edge of the Old Lead Belt — if {ancestor_name} crossed into St. Francois County for mining work, the records shift from agricultural to industrial, from county court to company ledger.
source_references: Missouri State Archives. Guide to County Records on Microfilm: Dent County.
Rafferty, Milton D. The Ozarks: Land and Life. 2nd ed. University of Arkansas Press, 2001.
Works Progress Administration. Historical Records Survey: Missouri County Records. Jefferson City, 1937-1942.
